blocks.content: They stood under the overhang and watched the rain come down.
Tommy Frazier appeared a few doors down, completely dry. He pointed at his hair. Then at theirs. He didn't need to say anything.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Okay. You were right.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I know.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: You can say it.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I don't need to say it.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Then what are you going to do with it?

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Hold it. Quietly. Forever.
Eli snorted. Savannah covered her mouth.
The rain was still going hard. The wet street was a mirror — streetlights early and amber in the storm-dark.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I didn't want to go back yet.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I know.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: That obvious?

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You were watching that sky for twenty minutes.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I was enjoying the view.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You were watching it turn green and thinking maybe just five more minutes.
He didn't say anything. She wasn't wrong.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I didn't want to go back yet either.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: You've been wanting to go up that tower for a while, haven't you.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Two summers.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Two summers and you never said.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I figured you'd get there eventually.
He looked at her for a second longer than he needed to.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Thanks for coming today.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You didn't ask us. We just came.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Still.
She looked at him then — something brief and specific in her expression — and turned back to the rain.
The storm was easing. The orange edge was coming in behind the clouds.
